<div class="mt-0.5 space-y-2.5">

<div class="eyebrow h-5 text-primary dark:text-primary-light text-sm font-semibold">

Propose Changes

</div>

# SEP Guidelines

<div class="mt-2 text-lg prose prose-gray dark:prose-invert [&>*]:[overflow-wrap:anywhere]">

Specification Enhancement Proposal (SEP) guidelines for proposing
changes to the Model Context Protocol

</div>

<div id="page-context-menu"
class="flex items-center shrink-0 min-w-[156px] mt-3 sm:hidden">

<div class="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-center font-medium">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="size-4" />Copy page

</div>

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="transition-transform text-gray-400 overflow-visible group-hover:text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-600 dark:group-hover:text-gray-400 rotate-90" />

</div>

<div id="content"
class="mdx-content @container/columns-container relative mt-8 prose prose-gray dark:prose-invert [contain:inline-size] isolate empty:hidden mb-14"
page-title="SEP Guidelines" page-href="/community/sep-guidelines">

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#what-is-a-sep"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">What is a SEP?</span>

<span data-as="p">SEP stands for Specification Enhancement Proposal. A
SEP is a design document providing information to the MCP community, or
describing a new feature for the Model Context Protocol or its
processes. The SEP should provide a concise technical specification of
the feature and a rationale for the feature.</span>
<span data-as="p">SEPs are the primary mechanism for proposing major new
features, collecting community input on an issue, and documenting the
design decisions that have gone into MCP. The SEP author is responsible
for building consensus within the community and documenting dissenting
opinions.</span> <span data-as="p">When drafting a SEP, authors should
review the
<a href="design-principles.html" class="link">MCP design principles</a>,
which outline the core values and tradeoffs that guide the protocol’s
evolution.</span> <span data-as="p">SEPs are maintained as markdown
files in the <a
href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/tree/main/seps"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer"><code>seps/</code>
directory</a> of the specification repository. Their revision history
serves as the historical record of the feature proposal.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#when-to-write-a-sep"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">When to Write a SEP</span>

<span data-as="p">The SEP process is reserved for changes that are
substantial enough to require broad community discussion, a formal
design document, and a historical record. A regular GitHub pull request
is often more appropriate for smaller changes.</span>
<span data-as="p">**Write a SEP if your change involves:**</span>

- **A new feature or protocol change** - Adding, modifying, or removing
  features in the protocol (new API methods, message format changes,
  interoperability standards)
- **A breaking change** - Any change that is not backwards-compatible
- **A governance or process change** - Altering decision-making or
  contribution guidelines
- **A complex or controversial topic** - Changes likely to have multiple
  valid solutions or generate significant debate

<span data-as="p">**Skip the SEP process for:**</span>

- Bug fixes and typo corrections
- Documentation clarifications
- Adding examples to existing features
- Minor schema fixes that don’t change behavior

<span data-as="p">Not sure? Ask in
<a href="communication.html#discord" class="link">Discord</a> before
starting significant work.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#sep-types"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">SEP Types</span>

<span data-as="p">There are three kinds of SEP:</span>

1.  **Standards Track** - Describes a new feature or implementation for
    the Model Context Protocol, or an interoperability standard
    supported outside the core specification.
2.  **Informational** - Describes a design issue or provides
    guidelines/information to the community without proposing a new
    feature.
3.  **Process** - Describes a process surrounding MCP or proposes a
    change to a process (like this document).

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#sep-workflow"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">SEP Workflow</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#step-by-step-process"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Step-by-Step Process</span>

<div class="callout my-4 px-5 py-4 overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex gap-3 border border-blue-200 bg-blue-50 dark:border-blue-900 dark:bg-blue-600/20"
callout-type="note">

<div class="mt-0.5 w-4" component-part="callout-icon">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="size-4 text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300" />

</div>

<div class="text-sm prose dark:prose-invert min-w-0 w-full [&_kbd]:bg-background-light dark:[&_kbd]:bg-background-dark [&_code]:!text-current [&_kbd]:!text-current [&_a]:!text-current [&_a]:border-current [&_strong]:!text-current text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300"
component-part="callout-content">

<span data-as="p">To improve your chances of a SEP being
accepted:</span>

- **Discuss your idea with the relevant
  <a href="working-interest-groups.html" class="link">working or interest
  group</a> in
  <a href="communication.html#discord" class="link">Discord</a> first.**
  This is the single best way to refine your proposal and build early
  support.
- **If no relevant group exists, start a conversation in <a
  href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/discussions"
  class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">GitHub Discussions</a>
  or the `#general` channel in
  <a href="communication.html#discord" class="link">Discord</a>.** If
  there is enough interest, it may be worth
  <a href="working-interest-groups.html#creating-an-interest-group"
  class="link">creating a new IG or WG</a> — the effort involved in
  finding sponsors and facilitators is a good signal of whether the idea
  has sufficient traction, and is still preferable to a cold submission.
- **Check alignment with
  <a href="governance.html#roles" class="link">Core Maintainer</a>
  priorities and
  <a href="design-principles.html" class="link">design principles</a>.**
  Priorities are generally reflected in the
  <a href="../development/roadmap.html" class="link">project roadmap</a>.
  Proposals outside current priorities or that conflict with design
  principles are more likely to face delays or additional friction in
  the review process.

</div>

</div>

1.  <span data-as="p">**Draft your SEP** as a markdown file named
    `0000-your-feature-title.md`, using `0000` as a placeholder. Follow
    the
    <a href="sep-guidelines.html#sep-format" class="link">SEP format</a>
    below.</span>
2.  <span data-as="p">**Create a pull request** adding your SEP file to
    the `seps/` directory in the
    <a href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol"
    class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">specification
    repository</a>.</span>
3.  <span data-as="p">**Update the SEP number**: Once your PR is
    created, rename the file using the PR number (e.g., PR \#1850
    becomes `1850-your-feature-title.md`) and update the SEP
    header.</span>
4.  <span data-as="p">**Find a Sponsor**: Tag a Core Maintainer or
    Maintainer from <a
    href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/blob/main/MAINTAINERS.md"
    class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">the maintainer list</a>.
    Choose someone whose area relates to your proposal. Tips:</span>
    - Tag 1-2 relevant maintainers, not everyone
    - Share your PR in the relevant Discord channel
    - If no response after 2 weeks, ask in `#general`
5.  <span data-as="p">**Sponsor assigns themselves**: When a sponsor
    agrees, they assign themselves to the PR and update the SEP status
    to `draft`.</span>
6.  <span data-as="p">**Informal review**: The sponsor reviews the
    proposal and may request changes. Discussion happens in PR
    comments.</span>
7.  <span data-as="p">**Formal review**: When ready, the sponsor updates
    the status to `in-review`. The SEP enters formal review by Core
    Maintainers (meetings every two weeks).</span>
8.  <span data-as="p">**Resolution**: The SEP may be `accepted`,
    `rejected`, or returned for revision. The sponsor updates the
    status.</span>
9.  <span data-as="p">**Finalization**: Once accepted, the reference
    implementation must be completed. When complete and incorporated
    into the specification, the sponsor updates the status to
    `final`.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#sep-statuses"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">SEP Statuses</span>

<div class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"
table-wrapper="true">

<div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table">

| Status       | Meaning                                          |
|--------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `draft`      | Has a sponsor, undergoing informal review        |
| `in-review`  | Ready for formal Core Maintainer review          |
| `accepted`   | Approved, awaiting reference implementation      |
| `rejected`   | Declined by Core Maintainers                     |
| `withdrawn`  | Author withdrew the proposal                     |
| `final`      | Complete with reference implementation           |
| `superseded` | Replaced by a newer SEP                          |
| `dormant`    | No sponsor found within 6 months; can be revived |

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">**Important distinction**: `dormant` is not the same
as `rejected`. A dormant SEP simply didn’t find a sponsor - the idea may
still be valid. If circumstances change (new community interest, new use
cases), a dormant SEP can be revived by finding a sponsor and reopening
the PR.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#sep-format"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">SEP Format</span>

<span data-as="p">Each SEP should have the following parts:</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#1-preamble"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">1. Preamble</span>

<span data-as="p">A short descriptive title, author names/contact info,
current status, SEP type, and PR number.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#2-abstract"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">2. Abstract</span>

<span data-as="p">A short (~200 word) description of the technical issue
being addressed.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#3-motivation"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">3. Motivation</span>

<span data-as="p">Why the existing protocol specification is inadequate.
This is critical - SEPs without sufficient motivation may be rejected
outright.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#4-specification"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">4. Specification</span>

<span data-as="p">The technical specification describing syntax and
semantics of the new feature. Must be detailed enough for competing,
interoperable implementations.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#5-rationale"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">5. Rationale</span>

<span data-as="p">Why particular design decisions were made, alternate
designs considered, and related work. Should provide evidence of
community consensus and address objections raised during
discussion.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#6-backward-compatibility"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">6. Backward Compatibility</span>

<span data-as="p">All SEPs introducing backward incompatibilities must
describe these incompatibilities, their severity, and how to deal with
them.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#7-reference-implementation"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">7. Reference Implementation</span>

<span data-as="p">Must be completed before the SEP reaches “Final”
status, but need not be complete before acceptance.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#8-security-implications"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">8. Security Implications</span>

<span data-as="p">Any security concerns related to the SEP should be
explicitly documented.</span> <span data-as="p">See the <a
href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/blob/main/seps/README.md#sep-file-structure"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">SEP template</a> for the
complete file structure.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#prototype-requirements"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Prototype Requirements</span>

<span data-as="p">Before a SEP can be accepted, you need “a prototype
implementation demonstrating the proposal.” Here’s what
qualifies:</span> <span data-as="p">**Acceptable prototypes:**</span>

- A working implementation in one of the official SDKs (as a
  branch/fork)
- A standalone proof-of-concept demonstrating the key mechanics
- Integration tests showing the proposed behavior
- A reference server or client implementing the feature

<span data-as="p">**The prototype should:**</span>

- Demonstrate the core functionality works as described
- Show the API design is practical and ergonomic
- Reveal any edge cases or implementation challenges
- Be runnable by reviewers (include setup instructions)

<span data-as="p">**Not sufficient:**</span>

- Pseudocode alone
- A design document without code
- “Trust me, it works” - reviewers need to see it

<span data-as="p">The prototype doesn’t need to be production-ready. It
exists to prove feasibility and surface issues early.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#the-sponsor-role"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">The Sponsor Role</span>

<span data-as="p">A Sponsor is a Core Maintainer or Maintainer who
champions the SEP through the review process. The sponsor’s
responsibilities include:</span>

- Reviewing the proposal and providing constructive feedback
- Requesting changes based on community input
- **Updating the SEP status** as the proposal progresses
- Initiating formal review when the SEP is ready
- Presenting and discussing the proposal at Core Maintainer meetings
- Ensuring the proposal meets quality standards

<span data-as="p">Authors should request status changes through their
sponsor rather than modifying the status field themselves.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#status-management"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Status Management</span>

<span data-as="p">**The Sponsor is responsible for updating the SEP
status.** This ensures status transitions are made by someone with the
authority and context to do so appropriately.</span>
<span data-as="p">The sponsor:</span>

1.  Updates the `Status` field directly in the SEP markdown file (or, if
    they do not have access to the source repo, work with the author to
    set the right status)
2.  Applies matching labels to the pull request (e.g., `draft`,
    `in-review`, `accepted`)

<span data-as="p">Both the markdown status field and PR labels should be
kept in sync. The markdown file is the canonical record (versioned with
the proposal), while PR labels make it easy to filter and search.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#sep-review-&amp;-resolution"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">SEP Review & Resolution</span>

<span data-as="p">SEPs are reviewed by the MCP Core Maintainers team
every two weeks.</span> <span data-as="p">For a SEP to be accepted it
must meet these criteria:</span>

- A prototype implementation demonstrating the proposal
- Clear benefit to the MCP ecosystem
- Community support and consensus

<span data-as="p">Once a SEP has been accepted, the reference
implementation must be completed. When complete and incorporated into
the main repository, the status changes to “Final”.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#after-rejection"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">After Rejection</span>

<span data-as="p">Rejection is not permanent. You can:</span>

1.  **Address the feedback** - If specific concerns were raised, address
    them and resubmit
2.  **Discuss the rejection** - Ask in Discord to understand the
    reasoning
3.  **Submit a competing SEP** - Sometimes a different approach works
    better
4.  **Wait for the right time** - Community needs evolve; what’s
    rejected today may be welcomed later

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#reporting-sep-bugs-or-updates"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Reporting SEP Bugs or Updates</span>

<span data-as="p">For SEPs not yet reaching `final` state, comment
directly on the SEP’s pull request. Once a SEP is finalized and merged,
submit updates by creating a new pull request that modifies the SEP
file.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="sep-guidelines.html#transferring-sep-ownership"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Transferring SEP Ownership</span>

<span data-as="p">It occasionally becomes necessary to transfer
ownership of SEPs to a new author. In general, we’d like to retain the
original author as a co-author, but that’s up to the original
author.</span> <span data-as="p">Good reasons to transfer
ownership:</span>

- Original author no longer has time or interest
- Original author is unreachable

<span data-as="p">Bad reasons:</span>

- You disagree with the direction (submit a competing SEP instead)
